classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "By this sentence, again, the apostle places in the same category those who have fellowship in the consciousness of evil.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: ‘Worthy of death,’ he says, ‘are both those who do such things, and those who consent with those that do them.’"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: I care not in what manner you have used these words, they are true.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And this is the substance of the teaching of the Catholic Church, that there is a great difference between those who consent because they take pleasure in such things, and those who tolerate while they dislike them.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: The former make themselves chaff, while they follow the barrenness of the chaff; the latter are the grain.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Let them wait for Christ, who bears the winnowing-fan, that they may be separated from the chaff.
